name: User abandons a half-drafted quote
description: User starts a quote, then says 'never mind, scrap that'. The draft must not silently linger in /quotes as a phantom draft.

persona.name: Diego Martínez
persona.context: Started talking through a job, customer texted back saying they went with someone else. Wants to abandon.

preconditions: Active draft quote in the current thread

steps.expect: Assistant confirms: 'Got it — discarding the draft. Anything else?' with a clear undo affordance
The just-cancelled draft is NOT in the active pipeline — either deleted or shown only in an Archived tab

steps.action: Returns to /assistant and types 'Wait, bring it back.'
steps.expect: If undo window still valid, the draft restores; otherwise a graceful 'too late, want to start over?'

evaluationCriteria.check: Cancellation confirmation is unmistakable, not a tiny grey toast
User feels in control — the app didn't quietly send something they meant to abandon
Backend marks the quote as discarded with a timestamp — auditable, not just hard-deleted
Toast/undo is reachable via keyboard within the announcement window
